definitions.technical: Rules specifying how stablecoin reserves must be spread across different asset types, custodians, and counterparties to reduce the risk that a single failure impairs the entire reserve portfolio.
definitions.lay: Rules requiring stablecoin issuers to spread their reserves across multiple banks, asset types, and business partners. The goal is to ensure that no single failure can wipe out a large share of the reserves backing the stablecoin.
definitions.parenthetical: (rules requiring reserve allocation across multiple asset types, custodians, and counterparties)
definitions.legal_significance: The NPRM offers two approaches: Option A (principles-based) and Option B (mandatory quantitative limits). The comment letter recommends Option B because its 40% concentration cap creates an auditable standard compatible with the monthly attestation process.
